text: Treating lung cancer can be challenging due to patient frailty and or need to receive additional treatments such as chemotherapy.
Minimally invasive technology by the surgeons at UW Health offers an effective alternative to conventional thoracotomy.
The video-assisted thoracoscopic lobectomy or VATS lobectomy is a novel approach in treating lung cancer.
Thoracic surgeons at UW Health are among only a few in Wisconsin who perform VATS lobectomies for patients with non-small cell lung cancers.
Learn about the benefits of the VATS lobectomy approach and see how this technique is providing patients with a shorter, less painful healing process.
Don't miss Minimally Invasive Options for Lung Cancer Patients, live June 9th at 5 o'clock Central.
